interpretations.description:
The Testament of Solomon
                
                    The "Testament of Solomon," a Greek text from the early centuries CE, provides the most
                    detailed account of the ring's use. It describes how Solomon used the ring to summon and
                    interrogate demons one by one, learning their names, powers, and weaknesses. Each demon
                    was then bound to a specific task in building the Temple or confined so it could harm
                    no one.
                

                Islamic Tradition
                
                    In Islamic tradition, Sulaiman (Solomon) was granted dominion over the jinn by Allah.
                    The ring allowed him to control winds, understand the speech of animals, and command
                    armies of spirits. The Quran describes how jinn worked for Solomon, building palaces
                    and diving for pearls. When he lost the ring briefly to a demon, his kingdom was thrown
                    into chaos until he recovered it.
                

                Western Occultism
                
                    Medieval grimoires such as the "Lesser Key of Solomon" claim to preserve Solomon's magical
                    knowledge. The hexagram or pentagram became known as the "Seal of Solomon" and was used
                    in ceremonial magic to invoke and constrain spirits. This tradition continues in modern
                    occultism and has profoundly influenced Western esotericism.

longDescription:
The Ring of Power That Commanded Demons and Spirits The Seal of Solomon is a legendary magical ring or signet that granted King Solomon
                    power over demons, jinn, and spirits. According to various traditions, the ring was
                    given to Solomon directly by God (or through the archangel Michael) and bore either
                    the ineffable Name of God or a sacred geometric symbol, granting him wisdom and
                    authority over the supernatural world.

extendedContent.content:
The Seal of Solomon is a legendary magical ring or signet that granted King Solomon
                    power over demons, jinn, and spirits. According to various traditions, the ring was
                    given to Solomon directly by God (or through the archangel Michael) and bore either
                    the ineffable Name of God or a sacred geometric symbol, granting him wisdom and
                    authority over the supernatural world.
                
                
                    With this ring, Solomon was said to have commanded legions of demons to help build
                    the Temple in Jerusalem, bound evil spirits, and gained knowledge of all things seen
                    and unseen. The ring became central to later magical traditions and grimoires claiming
                    to preserve Solomonic magic.
extendedContent.title: Description and Nature

extendedContent.content:

                    The ring allowed him to control winds, understand the speech of animals, and command

                    and diving for pearls. When he lost the ring briefly to a demon, his kingdom was thrown

                    knowledge. The hexagram or pentagram became known as the "Seal of Solomon" and was used
                    in ceremonial magic to invoke and constrain spirits. This tradition continues in modern
                    occultism and has profoundly influenced Western esotericism.
extendedContent.title: Legends and Traditions

shortDescription: The Ring of Power That Commanded Demons and Spirits
name: Seal of Solomon

sources.name: Testament of Solomon
sources.description: A pseudepigraphal text attributed to King Solomon, detailing his encounters with demons and the construction of the Temple. It is a primary source for many legends surrounding the Seal of Solomon.
sources.title: Testament of Solomon

sources.name: Talmud, Gittin 68a-b
sources.description: A section of the Babylonian Talmud containing a story about Solomon and the demon Asmodeus, a key narrative associated with the Seal's powers and limitations.
sources.title: Talmud, Gittin 68a-b

sources.name: Qur'an, Surah 34:12-13
sources.description: Verses in the Qur'an describing Solomon's (Sulaiman's) ability to command jinn to work for him, reflecting the Islamic perspective on his supernatural powers.
sources.title: Qur'an, Surah 34:12-13

sources.name: 1 Kings 11:1-13
sources.description: A passage from the Hebrew Bible describing Solomon's fall from grace due to his foreign wives and their gods, which some interpretations link to his reliance on magical practices and the Seal.
sources.title: 1 Kings 11:1-13

sources.name: Picatrix (Ghāyat al-Ḥakīm)
sources.description: An 11th-century grimoire of astrological magic, containing references to the Seal of Solomon and its use in summoning spirits and performing magical rituals.
sources.title: Picatrix (Ghāyat al-Ḥakīm)

description: The Ring of Power That Commanded Demons and Spirits

keyMyths.title: Solomon's Acquisition of the Seal
keyMyths.description: The Seal of Solomon, a ring or seal bearing the name of God, was given to King Solomon by God (or sometimes an angel) to aid him in his reign. It granted him power over demons, spirits, and other supernatural entities, enabling him to build the Temple and maintain his kingdom.

keyMyths.title: Construction of the Temple
keyMyths.description: Solomon used the power of the Seal to command demons to labor in the construction of the Temple in Jerusalem. He forced them to quarry stones, transport materials, and perform other tasks, showcasing his dominion over the supernatural realm.

keyMyths.title: Asmodeus and the Seal
keyMyths.description: One prominent narrative involves the demon Asmodeus, who either tricked Solomon into relinquishing the Seal temporarily or was subdued by Solomon using its power. This tale highlights the challenges and vulnerabilities associated with wielding such immense power.

keyMyths.title: Solomon's Fall from Grace
keyMyths.description: Some traditions suggest that Solomon's reliance on the Seal and his interactions with demons led to his spiritual downfall. He became enamored with foreign gods and practices, ultimately losing favor with God due to his misuse of the divine gift.

keyMyths.title: The Seal's Legacy
keyMyths.description: After Solomon's death, the Seal became a symbol of wisdom, authority, and control over the supernatural. It was sought after by magicians, alchemists, and others who desired to harness its power, leading to numerous legends and interpretations throughout history.
